violations.message: Null Reference: Avoid 'null'. Use 'Option' types to represent missing values.
Primitive Obsession: Do not use type aliases for primitives. Use Single-Case Discriminated Unions to make illegal states unrepresentable.
Parse, Don't Validate: Functions should return Result<ParsedType, Error> rather than a boolean validity flag.
Generic Catch: Do not catch generic exceptions for flow control. Use Result types instead.
Imperative Loops: Avoid 'while' loops. Use Seq.fold or recursion.
OOP Inheritance: Avoid OOP inheritance and interfaces. Use records of functions or Discriminated Unions.
Mutable Collections: Avoid C# mutable collections. Use F# immutable Map, Set, or list.
Mutation Overuse: Avoid 'mutable'. Use record copies with 'with' instead.
Null Reference: Avoid 'null'. Use 'Option' types to represent missing values.
